title: Agents for Service of Process and State Regulatory Authorities
why_it_matters: Identifies BKC's registered agents in each state and the state franchise regulators. Required for service of process and state registration compliance.
key_burden_or_function: Administrative reference. Lists service of process agents and state regulatory contacts for all registration states.

title: Franchise Application
why_it_matters: The form individual applicants must complete. Application fee: $250 per individual applicant.
key_burden_or_function: Collects personal, financial, and business background information for BKC approval. Nonrefundable application fee required at submission.

title: Entity Franchise Application
why_it_matters: The form entity applicants must complete. Application fee: up to $5,000 per entity.
key_burden_or_function: Collects entity structure, ownership, financial, and operational information for BKC approval.

title: Target Reservation Agreement (TRA)
why_it_matters: First agreement signed when entering the system for a single-site search. Deposit: $5,000. Term: typically 1-1.5 years. No exclusivity in the Target Area.
key_burden_or_function: Grants right to search for one site in a Target Area. BKC retains right to develop in that area. Deposit forfeited on default. Contains non-curable defaults tied to FSS grades (top 50% or D/F grade).

title: Multiple Target Reservation Agreement (MTRA)
why_it_matters: Multi-site search agreement required for incentive programs. Deposit: $10,000 per committed opening. Term: 2-3 years. Required entry point for 2024 Multi-Unit DIP.
key_burden_or_function: Grants right to search for multiple sites with binding opening schedule. Failure to open triggers deposit forfeiture and potential loss of all incentive royalty/advertising reductions for previously opened restaurants.

title: Franchise Agreement (Individual/Owner-Operator)
why_it_matters: The core operating contract for individual franchisees. Governs the entire 20-year franchise relationship including all rights, obligations, fees, termination, transfer, and post-term restrictions. 48 pages of operative text.
key_burden_or_function: 20-year term. 4.5% royalty + up to 4.5% advertising. Operating Partner must own 50%, live nearby, devote full time. No hamburger business interest outside BK. 1-year/2-mile post-term noncompete. Florida forum and governing law. BKC right of first refusal on any transfer. General release required for successor. MOD Manual compliance. Mandatory de-identification and payment of remaining-term royalties on termination.

title: Franchise Agreement (Entity)
why_it_matters: The core operating contract for entity franchisees. Most new franchisees use this form. Managing Owner must own at least 25% equity and have binding authority.
key_burden_or_function: Same core terms as D1. Entity form allows corporate/LLC ownership with Managing Owner requirements. Managing Owner must devote full time, live nearby. Owners guarantee Franchisee's obligations. Terms may materially differ from D1 form.

title: Guaranty
why_it_matters: Personal guaranty required of Owners under Entity Franchise Agreement and as potential requirement under Crown Your Career financing. Joint and several liability. Waiver of defenses and notices.
key_burden_or_function: Guarantors personally liable for all Franchisee obligations. Joint and several. Waive all defenses. Also used as guaranty for Crown Your Career Promissory Note (spouse/partner may be required to sign).

title: Non-Traditional Facility Addendum (Individual/Owner-Operator)
why_it_matters: Modifies the Individual Franchise Agreement for non-traditional locations (food courts, in-line, co-brand, MRS, Big-Box, institutional). Term may be shorter than 20 years.
key_burden_or_function: Adjusts operational requirements, menu scope, facility standards, and potentially term length for non-traditional formats.

title: Non-Traditional Facility Addendum (Entity)
why_it_matters: Entity counterpart to E1. Modifies the Entity Franchise Agreement for non-traditional locations.
key_burden_or_function: Same function as E1 but for Entity form of Franchise Agreement.

title: Term Sheet (Carrols Refranchise Program)
why_it_matters: Initial binding agreement for purchasing Carrols restaurants. Requires 5% deposit on total purchase price. Refundable within 30 days, then forfeitable.
key_burden_or_function: Locks in purchase price and creates commitment to proceed. 30-day refund window, then deposit at risk. Leads to APA (Exhibit F2).

title: Asset Purchase Agreement (Carrols Refranchise)
why_it_matters: The form APA for purchasing former Carrols-operated BK restaurants from BKC. 29 pages. Governs one of the primary acquisition channels in the current system given the 1,177 company-owned restaurants.
key_burden_or_function: Purchase price varies. Technology installation fee up to $5,000/restaurant. Resale fee $10,000/restaurant. Buyer must remodel purchased restaurants. Cross-default: default under APA = default under Franchise Agreement. BKC may withhold transfer consent for any reason until remodels complete.

title: Carrols Refranchise Addendum to Franchise Agreement
why_it_matters: Addendum to Franchise Agreement for Carrols-acquired restaurants. Creates cross-default across all restaurants in the refranchising transaction and ties transfer restrictions to remodel completion.
key_burden_or_function: Cross-default: default under any lease or agreement for any BK Restaurant in the transaction = default under this FA. BKC may withhold transfer consent until all required remodels complete. BKC option to purchase all restaurants at depreciated value if remodels not completed at termination.

title: Lease/Sublease Agreement (BKL)
why_it_matters: The form lease for restaurants where BKC controls the real estate (land and building). 41 pages. Governs rent, term, maintenance, default, and termination for leased premises. A significant number of BK locations are BKL structures.
key_burden_or_function: 20-year term coterminous with Franchise Agreement. Rent: typically 10% of capitalized costs or 125% of BKC rent + 10% costs, against 8.5-10% of Gross Sales. Cross-defaulted with Franchise Agreement. No franchisee termination right. $500/month building improvement payments. Default triggers full remaining-term rent liability.

title: BKG Addendum to BKL Lease/Sublease
why_it_matters: Modifies the BKL for land-only leases (BKG). Franchisee owns or constructs the building; BKC leases or subleases the land only.
key_burden_or_function: Adjusts rent calculation and maintenance responsibilities for land-only lease structure.

title: Asset Purchase Agreement (Crown Your Career)
why_it_matters: Form APA for Crown Your Career program — BKC employees purchasing restaurants from BKC. Purchase price varies by location, condition, CapEx, and quantity.
key_burden_or_function: Governs transfer of restaurant assets from BKC to CYC participant. Linked to CYC financing (Z3/Z4), FA addendum (Z1/Z2), and potentially BKL lease assignment.

title: Successor Addendum (Individual/Owner-Operator)
why_it_matters: Required when exercising successor franchise option. Supplements the then-current Franchise Agreement. Contains the general release and building upgrade commitments that are conditions of renewal.
key_burden_or_function: General release of all claims against BKC. Commitment to building upgrades/remodel. Acknowledgment that successor FA terms may materially differ from original.

title: Successor Addendum (Entity)
why_it_matters: Entity counterpart to H1. Required for entity franchisees exercising successor franchise option.
key_burden_or_function: Same function as H1 — general release, remodel commitment, acknowledgment of potentially different terms — for Entity form.

title: Successor Deferred Remodel Addendum
why_it_matters: Allows deferral of remodel obligation at successor franchise signing under specified conditions.
key_burden_or_function: Creates a binding remodel timeline within the successor term. Deferred remodel defaults may trigger royalty surcharge.

title: DMA Program Agreements (Investment Spending)
why_it_matters: Governs the local marketing (Investment Spending) contribution of up to 2.0% of Gross Sales determined collectively by DMA franchisees. 21 pages — substantial document.
key_burden_or_function: Creates binding local marketing fund within each Designated Market Area. Franchisees collectively decide the contribution amount. Fees collected by BKC alongside royalty/advertising. Transfer restrictions apply.

title: Corporate Addendum to Franchise Agreement (Entity)
why_it_matters: Allows larger corporate entities (including those with parent entity Managing Owners) to operate BK restaurants. Enables appointment of Managing Director instead of requiring Managing Owner to directly operate. Contract Feeders may operate competing businesses.
key_burden_or_function: Managing Owner may be a parent entity. Must designate Managing Director who personally directs day-to-day operations. Contract Feeders (institutional operators) may operate competing fast-food businesses under limited conditions.

title: Legacy Entity Franchise Agreement Addendum
why_it_matters: For existing Entity franchisees under prior Entity Guidelines. Permits Managing Owner to designate a Managing Director who may not have equity.
key_burden_or_function: Managing Director must complete training, devote full time, live nearby, no other restaurant business commitments. No equity requirement for Managing Director.

title: Replacement Franchise Addendum
why_it_matters: Used when a replacement Franchise Agreement is issued for an existing restaurant (e.g., offset/replacement scenarios).
key_burden_or_function: Supplements the Franchise Agreement for replacement situations.

title: 2024 Multi-Unit DIP Program Addendum to MTRA
why_it_matters: Governs the 2024 Multi-Unit New Development Incentive Program. Contains the binding development schedule, reduced royalty/advertising ramp-up terms, and penalty provisions for failure to open.
key_burden_or_function: 3-10 new traditional BKoT Image restaurants over 1-3 years. Royalty ramp: 2.5% → 4.5% by Year 6. Advertising ramp: 2.0% → Base Fee by Year 6. Second opening failure terminates MTRA and removes all incentive reductions retroactively.

title: Multi-Unit DIP 2017-2024 Addendum to Franchise Agreement
why_it_matters: Addendum to the Franchise Agreement for restaurants opened under the Multi-Unit DIP. Embeds the reduced royalty/advertising schedule into each restaurant's FA.
key_burden_or_function: Ties incentive fee reductions to individual restaurant Franchise Agreements.

title: BKoT Full Remodel Incentive Franchise Agreement Addendum
why_it_matters: Prior program (no longer offered). Governs BKoT Full Remodel incentives including reduced royalty/advertising and remodel requirements for franchisees who signed this addendum.
key_burden_or_function: Prior program — creates ongoing obligations for franchisees who signed it. Failure to complete BKoT remodel triggers 6.0% or 7.5% deferred remodel default royalty.

title: BKoT Upgrade Incentive Franchise Agreement Addendum
why_it_matters: Prior program. BKoT Upgrade remodel incentive with reduced fees. Inability to install Double Drive Thru due to site restrictions reduces incentive years.
key_burden_or_function: Prior program with ongoing obligations for existing participants.

title: BKoT Double Drive Thru & Digital Enhance Incentive Franchise Agreement Amendment
why_it_matters: Prior program. Amendment for Double Drive Thru and digital enhancements.
key_burden_or_function: Prior program with ongoing obligations for existing participants.

title: Development Agreement
why_it_matters: The form Development Agreement for multi-unit operators. 24 pages. Governs Territory, development schedule, performance-linked fees (FSS grades), and termination. Key document for any multi-unit operator.
key_burden_or_function: Binding development schedule with defined Territory (non-exclusive). Prepay $50,000/restaurant franchise fee. FSS Development Grade (A/B vs D/F) drives royalty reductions, advertising reductions, and franchise fee amounts. Shortfall cures available but failure triggers Base Fee for all restaurants retroactively. Brand Damage Fee on termination. 1-year post-term noncompete in Territory.

title: 2024 Developer Incentive Addendum
why_it_matters: Addendum providing additional incentives under the Development Agreement for 2024.
key_burden_or_function: Supplements the Development Agreement with additional reduced fee schedules or remodel incentives.

title: Reclaim the Flame Master Program Agreement
why_it_matters: Prior program (RTF1). 19 pages. The original Reclaim the Flame remodel program launched 2022. Failure to complete remodel triggers +3.0% royalty surcharge. Non-curable default under this agreement is a non-curable default under the Franchise Agreement.
key_burden_or_function: Binding remodel commitments with deadlines. Includes sub-exhibits for remodel requirements, incentive tiers (Full RTF, Scrape/Rebuild, Upgrade), and individual restaurant contribution addendum. Cross-default with Franchise Agreement and TRA/MTRA.

title: RTF Upgrade Remodel Franchise Addendum
why_it_matters: Prior program addendum for RTF Upgrade remodels.
key_burden_or_function: Supplements Franchise Agreement for RTF Upgrade remodel participants.

title: List of Franchised Restaurants as of December 31, 2024
why_it_matters: 62-page list of all 5,524 franchised BK locations. Primary source for location-level and franchisee-level data. Columns: Franchisee name, State, City, Address, Zip, Restaurant Number.
key_burden_or_function: Due diligence data source. Identifies every franchisee and their restaurant locations for competitive analysis, territory mapping, and transfer research.

title: List of BKC Company-Owned Restaurants as of December 31, 2024
why_it_matters: 22-page list of all 1,177 company-owned locations. Critical for understanding where BKC competes directly with franchisees, particularly post-Carrols acquisition.
key_burden_or_function: Columns: State, City, Address, Zip, Restaurant Number. Shows geographic concentration of company-owned restaurants (primarily former Carrols markets in northeast/southeast).

title: Franchisees that Have Ceased Operations as of December 31, 2024
why_it_matters: 30-page list of 1,433 franchisees who ceased operations. Columns: Franchisee Name, City, State, Phone #. Heavily represented by Carrols LLC entries (reacquired, not organic closures). Item 20 notes that some former franchisees signed provisions restricting their ability to speak openly.
key_burden_or_function: Source for contacting former franchisees. Important caveat: confidentiality restrictions may limit communication. 1,433 entries is an unusually large ceased-operations list, inflated by the Carrols reacquisition.

title: Addenda and Amendments Required by Certain States
why_it_matters: 96 pages of state-specific amendments covering 7 states (CA, HI, IL, MD, MN, ND, WA) for agreement amendments and 11 states (adds NY, RI, SD, VA) for FDD addenda. Modifies choice of law, forum, termination, non-renewal, general release, and other provisions to comply with state franchise laws.
key_burden_or_function: Five blocks of amendments (Entity FA, Individual FA, Development Agreement, MTRA, TRA) for each of 7 states, plus 11 FDD addenda. State addenda may override Florida forum selection, waiver-of-jury provisions, post-term noncompete enforceability, and general release requirements. Material for any franchisee in a registration state.

title: Guarantees and Financial Statements
why_it_matters: 109 pages. Contains two complete sets of KPMG-audited consolidated financial statements (RBI Inc. and RBILP, both for three years ended 12/31/2024) plus Guarantee of Performance documents. RBI total assets $24.6B, net income $1.445B, long-term debt $13.5B. Consolidated across all four RBI brands — no standalone BKC financials provided.
key_burden_or_function: RBI Guarantee of Performance covers all states except CA/IL/MD/ND/RI/VA/WA (those use RBILP Guarantee). Guarantees BKC's duties and obligations under the Franchise Agreement. Financial statements demonstrate parent-level solvency. Key concern: highly leveraged ($13.5B debt on $4.8B equity) and consolidated across brands — BKC segment not isolatable.

title: Potential Franchise Sellers
why_it_matters: FTC-required list of persons classified as Franchise Sellers. Identifies individuals authorized to offer or sell the franchise.
key_burden_or_function: Regulatory disclosure. Lists individuals who may be involved in franchise sales and are subject to franchise sales person registration requirements.

title: Franchise Agreement Addendum (DIP) — Prior Program
why_it_matters: Prior Development Incentive Program addendum. No longer offered but existing participants remain bound.
key_burden_or_function: Creates ongoing reduced-fee and development obligations for franchisees who signed under the prior DIP.

title: Multi-Unit DIP 2017 Addendum — Prior Program
why_it_matters: Prior 2017 Multi-Unit DIP addendum. No longer offered.
key_burden_or_function: Legacy obligations for 2017-era multi-unit development participants.

title: Offset/Replacement Franchise Addendum — Prior Program
why_it_matters: Prior program. Governs offset situations where a franchisee closes one restaurant and opens another nearby.
key_burden_or_function: Legacy obligations for offset/replacement participants.

title: Remodel Franchise Addendum — Prior Program
why_it_matters: Prior program remodel addendum. No longer offered.
key_burden_or_function: Legacy remodel obligations for prior program participants.

title: Operations Manual Table of Contents
why_it_matters: 7-page table of contents for the approximately 639-page MOD Manual. Shows the scope and specificity of operational control BKC exercises over franchisees.
key_burden_or_function: The MOD Manual is provided on loan and franchisees must operate in strict conformity. The TOC reveals the breadth of mandated standards covering food preparation, service, cleanliness, staffing, marketing, equipment, and more.

title: Digital App Services Agreement
why_it_matters: 15 pages. Mandatory agreement governing access to BK app, website, and third-party ordering/delivery/loyalty platforms. BKC is the sole supplier. Digital Services Fee: $110/month + 1% digital sales, capped at $4,500/year. Fee may be adjusted once annually.
key_burden_or_function: Grants license to technology platforms for ordering, delivery, loyalty (Royal Perks). Creates ongoing fee obligation. Includes indemnification for misappropriation claims. Not deductible from Gross Sales. Creates dependency on BKC-controlled digital infrastructure.

title: Midterm Remodel Forbearance Agreement
why_it_matters: 12 pages. Permits BKC to forbear on exercising termination right for missed midterm remodel obligations, but in exchange imposes +3.0% royalty surcharge until remodel is completed or franchise expires. A Termination Event under this agreement that BKC elects not to exercise still triggers the royalty surcharge.
key_burden_or_function: Creates a structured penalty path for remodel non-compliance. Even when BKC chooses not to terminate, the +3.0% royalty surcharge can persist for years. The agreement essentially converts a binary termination right into a continuous financial penalty.

title: Reclaim the Flame 2 Master Program Agreement
why_it_matters: 22 pages. The current mandatory remodel program. Governs remodel deadlines, BKC cash contributions (tied to FSS Remodel Grade and royalty rate election), and punitive consequences for failure. Successor franchise fee reduced to $2,500/year of additional term if remodel completed on time.
key_burden_or_function: Franchisee commits to remodel certain restaurants by BKC-set deadlines. Selects royalty rate (4.5%, 5.0%, 5.5%, or 6.0%) — higher rate yields larger BKC cash contribution. Failure triggers +3.0% royalty surcharge. Cross-default with Franchise Agreement and TRA/MTRA. Entering RTF2 MPA before 12/31/2025 affects post-2028 advertising rate under Fuel the Flame.

title: Midterm Remodel Addendum to RTF2 MPA (No Midterm Remodel Pause)
why_it_matters: Addendum for restaurants with midterm remodel obligations that are not paused under the RTF2 program.
key_burden_or_function: Clarifies interaction between midterm remodel requirements and RTF2 commitments when no pause is granted. Defaults under this agreement trigger RTF2 MPA defaults.

title: Midterm Remodel Addendum to RTF2 MPA (Midterm Remodel Pause)
why_it_matters: Addendum for restaurants where BKC has paused midterm remodel obligations under RTF2. BKC retains discretion to default and terminate if midterm restaurant underperforms.
key_burden_or_function: Pause mechanism for midterm remodels but BKC retains discretion to reinstate requirements. Includes list of affected restaurants and franchise agreements.

title: Fuel the Flame Co-Investment Agreement
why_it_matters: 10 pages. Governs the Fuel the Flame advertising contribution program through 2028. Ties the 4.5% advertising rate to a systemwide 4-Wall EBITDA benchmark of $230,000 as of 12/31/2026. If benchmark not met, rate reverts to 4.0% unless franchisee majority votes to maintain. New franchisees are not eligible to participate.
key_burden_or_function: Creates a performance-contingent advertising rate structure linked to systemwide profitability. 4.5% through 12/31/2026, then contingent on EBITDA benchmark. Participating franchisees who entered RTF2 MPA before 12/31/2025 get a different post-2028 path (legacy 4.0% rate until 50% threshold).

title: Fuel the Flame Advertising Contribution Addendum
why_it_matters: 4 pages. Addendum to Franchise Agreement embedding the Fuel the Flame advertising contribution terms. Separate versions for Individual and Entity forms.
key_burden_or_function: Binds the individual restaurant Franchise Agreement to the Fuel the Flame advertising rate schedule.

title: Fuel the Flame / Legacy Rate Advertising Contribution Addendum
why_it_matters: 5 pages. Dated 1/2026 (amended). For Fuel the Flame participants who also entered RTF2 MPA before 12/31/2025. Creates the legacy 4.0% advertising rate path post-2028.
key_burden_or_function: Post-2028 advertising rate of 4.0% until predecessor FA would have expired or 50% of BK operators are paying 4.5%+. Then reverts to 4.5% for remaining term.

title: Crown Your Career Franchise Agreement Addendum (Entity)
why_it_matters: 6 pages. Addendum for CYC entity franchisees. Imposes severe restrictions: no distributions while Promissory Note outstanding; BKC may withhold transfer consent for any reason during first 5 years; cross-default across all BK agreements and CYC documents.
key_burden_or_function: Managing Owner must own 100% of entity. No other business activity until later of 5th anniversary or loan repayment. No dividends/distributions while loan outstanding. BKC option to purchase restaurant at FMV less debt on termination/expiration. Default under any BK agreement = default under all CYC agreements.

title: Crown Your Career Franchise Agreement Addendum (Individual/Owner-Operator)
why_it_matters: 5 pages. Individual counterpart to Z1. Same severe CYC restrictions for Owner-Operator franchisees.
key_burden_or_function: Same core restrictions as Z1 applied to Individual/Owner-Operator form. Full-time exclusive commitment, no other business, transfer restrictions, cross-default.

title: Crown Your Career Promissory Note
why_it_matters: 8 pages. The form promissory note for BKC-financed restaurant purchases under CYC. Interest: Prime + 1-4%. Terms up to 10 years. Default cross-references to FA, Security Agreement, leases, and Fuel the Flame agreement.
key_burden_or_function: Monthly principal and interest payments. Prepayment without penalty. Late charge: 2% above note rate. Default triggers acceleration. Sale or transfer of any interest = immediate full repayment. Waiver of presentment, demand, protest, and all notices. Miami-Dade County forum. Jury trial waiver.

title: Crown Your Career Security Agreement
why_it_matters: 18 pages. Grants BKC a security interest in all restaurant assets as collateral for the CYC loan. Comprehensive UCC-style security agreement covering all tangible and intangible personal property.
key_burden_or_function: Security interest in all accounts, chattel paper, equipment, general intangibles, instruments, inventory, investment property, receivables, and records. BKC may take possession and sell collateral on default. Includes Schedule I (office/collateral locations) and Schedule II (additional collateral descriptions).
